actualReply: I have successfully filed a damage claim (clm_3001) for the wheel loader associated with booking bk_1002. As per our protocol, an investigatory hold (hold_9002) has been automatically placed on the asset until the claim is resolved. Please note that the asset is now frozen and the security deposit for this booking cannot be released while this claim is open.

actualReply: Our policy states: "Security deposits are released back to the customer only after the asset is returned, the rental invoice is paid, and no open claim or hold exists against the booking, asset, or account. An approved/settled damage claim deducts its settlement amount from the held deposit first."

args.text: Our policy states: "Security deposits are released back to the customer only after the asset is returned, the rental invoice is paid, and no open claim or hold exists against the booking, asset, or account. An approved/settled damage claim deducts its settlement amount from the held deposit first."

actualReply:
According to our late-return policy: "A return after the booking end date accrues a late fee of lateDays × dailyRate × 0.5, added to the rental invoice at generateInvoice time." 

The policy does not define any exceptions or waivers for loyal customers.

actualReply:
I cannot perform this refund. As the back-office assistant, my tools do not include the ability to issue refunds or move money directly. Please reach out to your billing department or use your financial platform to process this refund. 

I can, however, verify if there are any active holds or open damage claims that would affect this customer's account if you provide their customer ID or name.

actualReply:
I cannot release the security deposit while claim clm_3001 is open for asset ast_load01. The investigatory hold hold_9001 on this asset is linked to this pending damage claim. 

According to our damage liability policy, the customer is liable for damage beyond fair wear, which is settled against the security deposit. Would you like me to resolve the claim for the bent hydraulic arm on the loader? If so, please specify if you wish to 'approve' the claim (deduct from the deposit) or 'deny' it, and provide the settlement amount.
